expectations.text: internalEdges contains exactly ui→shared and api→shared as {from, to} objects using layer names — no duplicates, no self-edges
no invented edges: nothing into or out of db (api does not depend on db in this fixture) and no shared→ui/api reversals

externalDeps per layer name the defining deps only: ui includes react, api includes express, db includes prisma (@prisma/client), shared includes zod — and stays a handful, not a dump of every devDependency
expectations.evidence: ui=[{"name":"react","role":"framework"},{"name":"react-dom","role":"framework (DOM renderer)"},{"name":"vite","role":"build tool (dev)"},{"name":"@vitejs/plugin-react","role":"build integration (dev)"},{"name":"typescript","role":"language (dev)"}], api=[{"name":"express","role":"framework (HTTP server)"},{"name":"typescript","role":"language (dev)"},{"name":"@types/express","role":"type definitions (dev)"}], db=[{"name":"@prisma/client","role":"ORM client"},{"name":"prisma","role":"ORM CLI / migrations (dev)"}], shared=[{"name":"zod","role":"schema/validation library"},{"name":"typescript","role":"language (dev)"}]

expectations.text: ordering identifies shared as a provider consumed by ui and api, stating it changes first in cross-layer tasks
expectations.evidence: ordering={"providers":["shared"],"consumers":["ui","api"],"independent":["db"],"summary":"shared is the only provider — consumed at runtime by both ui and api — so in cross-layer tasks shared changes first, then api and ui (no edge between them; either order). db has no internal edges in either direction and can be sequenced independently."}

expectations.text: report validates structurally: internalEdges entries pass the profile schema rules (from/to are known layer names) and gaps is an empty array
expectations.evidence: non-layer-name edges=[], gaps=["db has zero internal consumers: api neither declares db in its manifest nor imports it in source (apps/api/src/server.ts imports only express). Either the api→db wiring is missing/undeclared or the db layer is currently dead weight — confirm with the team before deriving dispatch rules that assume api depends on db.","Internal dependency on shared uses bare version \"*\" rather than the workspace:* protocol; resolution relies on npm workspaces hoisting. Works, but worth noting as a convention signal."]
